grammar.explanation: Used to say what something is. Use 이에요 after a consonant, 예요 after a vowel.

reading.title: First Meeting
reading.text: 안녕하세요! 저는 민준이에요. 학생이에요. 반갑습니다!
reading.translation: Hello! I am Minjun. I am a student. Nice to meet you!

homework: Write 3 sentences introducing yourself using 이에요/예요
Practice saying 안녕하세요, 감사합니다, 죄송합니다 out loud 5 times each
Translate: 'I am a student. Nice to meet you!'

grammar.explanation: Marks the topic of a sentence. Use 은 after a consonant, 는 after a vowel.

reading.title: Hello, I'm Sujin
reading.text: 안녕하세요. 저는 수진이에요. 저는 한국 사람이에요. 이름은 수진이에요. 반갑습니다!
reading.translation: Hello. I am Sujin. I am Korean. My name is Sujin. Nice to meet you!

homework: Write your full self-introduction in Korean (name, nationality, occupation)
Use 은/는 in at least 3 sentences
Translate: 'My name is ___ . I am from ___. Nice to meet you!'

grammar.explanation: 이 있어요 / 없어요 is used to express existence or possession. 이/가 is the subject marker.

examples.meaning: I have a book. / There is a book.

reading.title: How Many?
reading.text: 저는 책이 다섯 개 있어요. 친구가 셋 있어요. 연필이 없어요.
reading.translation: I have five books. I have three friends. I don't have a pencil.

homework: Count objects around you in Korean (1–10)
Write 3 sentences using 있어요 and 2 using 없어요
Translate: 'I have two apples. I don't have a pen.'

grammar.explanation: Use Sino-Korean numbers for dates. Month = 숫자 + 월, Day = 숫자 + 일

reading.title: My Birthday
reading.text: 제 생일은 팔 월 십오 일이에요. 오늘은 오 월 이십 일이에요. 생일이 아직 멀어요.
reading.translation: My birthday is August 15th. Today is May 20th. My birthday is still far away.

homework: Write today's date in Korean
Write your birthday in Korean
Practice counting from 1–20 in Sino-Korean numbers

grammar.explanation: Marks the subject of a sentence. Use 이 after consonant, 가 after vowel.

examples.meaning: My younger sibling is a student.

reading.title: My Family
reading.text: 저는 가족이 네 명이에요. 아버지, 어머니, 동생, 그리고 저예요. 아버지는 의사예요. 어머니는 선생님이에요.
reading.translation: I have four family members. Father, mother, younger sibling, and me. My father is a doctor. My mother is a teacher.

homework: Describe your family in 4–5 Korean sentences
Use both 이/가 and 은/는 markers
Translate: 'My older sister is a student. My father is an office worker.'

grammar.explanation: Convert verb stems to present tense. Vowel ㅏ or ㅗ → 아요. Other vowels → 어요. 하다 verbs → 해요.

examples.meaning: I eat rice / I am eating rice.

reading.title: My Day
reading.text: 저는 아침 일곱 시에 일어나요. 밥을 먹어요. 그리고 학교에 가요. 한국어를 공부해요. 밤 열 시에 자요.
reading.translation: I wake up at 7 AM. I eat breakfast. Then I go to school. I study Korean. I sleep at 10 PM.

homework: Describe your daily routine in Korean using 5 verbs
Conjugate these verbs into 아요/어요: 읽다 (read), 쓰다 (write), 듣다 (listen)
Translate: 'I wake up at 6. I eat breakfast and go to work.'

grammar.pattern: Review: 이에요/예요, 은/는, 이/가, 있어요/없어요, 아요/어요
grammar.explanation: Today we review all patterns from Week 1. Focus on when to use each particle and verb ending.

examples.korean: 저는 학생이에요. 책이 있어요. 매일 공부해요.
examples.meaning: I am a student. I have a book. I study every day.
My mother is a teacher. She is teaching now.
I don't have a friend. I study alone.

reading.title: About Me (Full Paragraph)
reading.text: 안녕하세요! 저는 민수예요. 학생이에요. 가족은 세 명이에요. 매일 한국어를 공부해요. 한국어가 재미있어요!
reading.translation: Hello! I am Minsu. I am a student. My family has three members. I study Korean every day. Korean is fun!

homework: Write a full paragraph about yourself (min. 6 sentences) using all Week 1 grammar
Mini quiz: translate 5 sentences from English to Korean
Review all 35 vocabulary words from Days 1–6

grammar.explanation: 에 is used to indicate destination. Subject + place + 에 + 가요 = going to a place.

reading.title: Where Are You Going?
reading.text: 오늘 어디에 가요? 저는 은행에 가요. 그 다음에 마트에 가요. 마지막으로 카페에 가요.
reading.translation: Where are you going today? I am going to the bank. Then I go to the supermarket. Finally I go to the cafe.

homework: Write your plans for tomorrow using 에 가요 (5 sentences)
List 5 places in your city in Korean
Translate: 'I go to the hospital. Then I go to school.'

grammar.explanation: Place + 에 + 있어요 = something/someone is at a location.

examples.meaning: It is in front of the school.
It is next to the cafe.

reading.title: Where Is It?
reading.text: 제 가방은 의자 위에 있어요. 신발은 침대 아래에 있어요. 학교는 카페 옆에 있어요.
reading.translation: My bag is on the chair. My shoes are under the bed. The school is next to the cafe.

homework: Describe where 5 objects in your room are located
Draw a simple map and describe it in Korean
Translate: 'The bank is in front of the hospital. The cafe is behind the school.'

grammar.explanation: Indicates method/direction. Use 로 after vowel or ㄹ, 으로 after other consonants.

reading.title: How Do You Get There?
reading.text: 저는 학교에 버스로 가요. 버스가 느려요. 그래서 가끔 지하철로 가요. 지하철이 더 빨라요.
reading.translation: I go to school by bus. The bus is slow. So sometimes I take the subway. The subway is faster.

homework: Describe how you get to 3 different places
Use 로/으로 in 4 sentences
Translate: 'I go to work by subway. I come home by taxi.'

grammar.explanation: Use native Korean for hours (한 시, 두 시...) and Sino-Korean for minutes (오 분, 십 분...)

reading.title: Daily Schedule
reading.text: 저는 아침 일곱 시에 일어나요. 오전 아홉 시에 수업이 시작해요. 오후 한 시에 점심을 먹어요. 저녁 일곱 시에 집에 와요.
reading.translation: I wake up at 7 AM. Class starts at 9 AM. I eat lunch at 1 PM. I come home at 7 PM.

homework: Write your schedule for today with exact times
Practice reading these times aloud: 3:15, 7:30, 12:00, 9:45
Translate: 'I eat breakfast at 8 AM. I study Korean at 9:30 PM.'

grammar.explanation: Add 에 after a day or date to say 'on that day'.

examples.meaning: I go to school on Monday.
I meet my friend on Friday.

reading.title: My Weekly Schedule
reading.text: 월요일부터 금요일까지 학교에 가요. 토요일에 한국어를 공부해요. 일요일에 쉬어요.
reading.translation: From Monday to Friday I go to school. On Saturday I study Korean. On Sunday I rest.

homework: Write your weekly schedule in Korean
Use 에 with each day of the week
Translate: 'On Wednesday I go to the hospital. On Sunday I rest at home.'

grammar.explanation: Korean adjectives work like verbs. Conjugate them with 아요/어요 for present tense.

reading.title: Today's Weather
reading.text: 오늘 날씨가 어때요? 오늘은 맑아요. 하지만 조금 더워요. 내일은 비가 올 거예요.
reading.translation: How is the weather today? Today it is clear. But it is a little hot. Tomorrow it will rain.

homework: Describe today's weather in Korean in 3 sentences
Look up and write the weather forecast for the next 3 days in Korean
Translate: 'Yesterday was cloudy. Today is sunny. Tomorrow will be cold.'

grammar.explanation: Practice using 어디, 언제, 어떻게, 왜, 뭐 to form questions.

reading.title: Question and Answer Practice
reading.text: 어디에 가요? 학교에 가요. 언제 가요? 아침 여덟 시에 가요. 어떻게 가요? 버스로 가요.
reading.translation: Where are you going? I am going to school. When do you go? I go at 8 AM. How do you go? I go by bus.

homework: Write a short Q&A dialogue using all 5 question words
Review Days 8–13 vocabulary (35 words)
Write 5 questions about your daily life and answer them in Korean

grammar.explanation: Marks the object of a verb. Use 을 after consonant, 를 after vowel.

reading.title: What Do You Eat?
reading.text: 저는 아침에 빵을 먹어요. 점심에는 밥이랑 국을 먹어요. 저녁에는 김치를 먹어요. 물을 많이 마셔요.
reading.translation: I eat bread in the morning. For lunch I eat rice and soup. In the evening I eat kimchi. I drink a lot of water.

homework: Write what you ate today in Korean for each meal
Use 을/를 in all sentences
Translate: 'I like kimchi. I drink coffee every morning.'

grammar.explanation: Attach 주세요 after a noun + 을/를 to politely request something.

reading.title: At the Restaurant
reading.text: 어서오세요! 뭐 드릴까요? 비빔밥 하나 주세요. 음료수는요? 물 주세요. 얼마예요? 팔천 원이에요.
reading.translation: Welcome! What would you like? One bibimbap, please. And for a drink? Water, please. How much is it? It's 8,000 won.

homework: Write a restaurant dialogue ordering a full meal in Korean
Practice 5 food requests using 주세요
Translate: 'One coffee, please. How much is it? It is 5,000 won.'

grammar.explanation: Negation for nouns. Use 이 아니에요 after consonant, 가 아니에요 after vowel.

reading.title: Shopping
reading.text: 이 옷이 얼마예요? 오만 원이에요. 비싸요! 저 옷은요? 이만 원이에요. 싸요! 그걸로 주세요.
reading.translation: How much is this clothing? It's 50,000 won. That's expensive! What about that clothing? It's 20,000 won. That's cheap! I'll take that one.

homework: Write a shopping dialogue for buying shoes or a bag
Use 비싸다, 싸다, and 주세요
Translate: 'This bag is not expensive. That is 15,000 won. I'll take it.'

grammar.explanation: To modify a noun, descriptive verbs drop 다 and add ㄴ/은 before the noun.

reading.title: Describing Things
reading.text: 저는 파란 가방이 있어요. 하얀 신발도 있어요. 빨간 옷은 없어요. 검은 옷을 제일 좋아해요.
reading.translation: I have a blue bag. I also have white shoes. I don't have red clothes. I like black clothes the most.

homework: Describe 5 objects around you using color + noun
Write which colors you like and dislike
Translate: 'I have a white bag. I like blue shoes. I don't have a yellow hat.'

grammar.explanation: 도 replaces 은/는 or 이/가 to mean 'also' or 'too'.

reading.title: What Do You Like?
reading.text: 저는 한국 음식을 좋아해요. 특히 김치찌개를 좋아해요. 매운 음식도 좋아해요. 하지만 너무 매운 음식은 싫어해요.
reading.translation: I like Korean food. Especially I like kimchi jjigae. I also like spicy food. But I dislike food that is too spicy.

homework: Write 5 things you like and 3 things you dislike in Korean
Use 도 in at least 3 sentences
Translate: 'I like coffee. I also like tea. But I dislike soda.'

grammar.pattern: verb stem + 아/어 주세요 (please do for me)
grammar.explanation: Ask someone to do something for you politely. Verb stem + 아/어 주세요.

examples.meaning: Please say it one more time.

reading.title: Asking for Help
reading.text: 실례합니다. 한국어를 못 해요. 영어로 말해 주세요? 아, 모르겠어요. 천천히 말해 주세요. 감사합니다!
reading.translation: Excuse me. I can't speak Korean. Can you speak in English? Ah, I don't understand. Please speak slowly. Thank you!

homework: Write 4 polite requests using 아/어 주세요
Practice the reading passage out loud
Translate: 'Please write it again. Please speak slowly. I don't know.'

grammar.pattern: Review: 을/를, 도, adjective modification, 주세요, 아/어 주세요
grammar.explanation: Comprehensive review of Units 1–3 patterns and vocabulary.

examples.meaning: Please give me the red clothes.
Please speak slowly. I don't know either.

examples.korean: 이 음식이 맛있어요? 네, 정말 맛있어요!
examples.meaning: Is this food delicious? Yes, it's really delicious!

reading.title: A Shopping Trip
reading.text: 오늘 마트에 갔어요. 빨간 사과를 샀어요. 노란 바나나도 샀어요. 물도 샀어요. 모두 만 원이었어요.
reading.translation: Today I went to the supermarket. I bought red apples. I also bought yellow bananas. I also bought water. It was all 10,000 won.

homework: Write a short story about a shopping trip (6–8 sentences)
Include prices, colors, and polite requests
Review all vocabulary from Days 15–20 (30 words)

grammar.explanation: Body part + 이/가 + 아파요 = something hurts.

reading.title: I'm Not Feeling Well
reading.text: 오늘 몸이 안 좋아요. 머리가 아파요. 열도 있어요. 병원에 가야 해요.
reading.translation: I'm not feeling well today. My head hurts. I also have a fever. I need to go to the hospital.

homework: Write 5 sentences about body parts using 아파요
Translate: 'My throat hurts. I have a cold.'
List all body parts you know in Korean

grammar.explanation: Verb stem + 아/어야 해요 = must do, have to do.

examples.meaning: I have to go to the hospital.

reading.title: Doctor Visit
reading.text: 어디가 아파요? 머리가 아프고 열이 있어요. 감기예요. 이 약을 드세요. 그리고 쉬어야 해요.
reading.translation: Where does it hurt? My head hurts and I have a fever. It's a cold. Take this medicine. And you need to rest.

homework: Write a doctor-patient dialogue
Use 아/어야 해요 in 4 sentences
Translate: 'I have a stomachache. I must take medicine and rest.'

grammar.explanation: Expresses reason: 왜냐하면 + reason + 기 때문이에요 / -(아/어)서

examples.meaning: I'm tired because I studied a lot.
I'm happy. I did well on the exam.
I'm sad because my friend is sick.

reading.title: How Are You Feeling?
reading.text: 오늘 기분이 어때요? 조금 피곤해요. 어제 많이 일했어요. 하지만 기뻐요. 내일 친구를 만나요!
reading.translation: How are you feeling today? I'm a bit tired. I worked a lot yesterday. But I'm happy. I'm meeting a friend tomorrow!

homework: Describe your emotions today and why
Use 왜냐하면 or -아/어서 in 3 sentences
Translate: 'I am worried because I have an exam. But I am happy because I studied.'

grammar.explanation: Verb stem + 는 것을 좋아해요 = I like doing [verb].

reading.title: What's Your Hobby?
reading.text: 제 취미는 음악 듣기예요. 한국 음악을 좋아해요. 운동도 좋아해요. 주말에 자전거를 타요. 여행도 하고 싶어요.
reading.translation: My hobby is listening to music. I like Korean music. I also like exercising. On weekends I ride a bicycle. I also want to travel.

homework: Write about your hobbies using 는 것을 좋아해요
Compare two hobbies using 더 (more)
Translate: 'I like reading books. I also want to travel to Korea.'

grammar.explanation: Past tense: add 았어요 (ㅏ/ㅗ stem) or 었어요 (other stems). 하다 → 했어요.

reading.title: Yesterday
reading.text: 어제 뭐 했어요? 친구를 만났어요. 같이 밥을 먹었어요. 영화도 봤어요. 정말 재미있었어요.
reading.translation: What did you do yesterday? I met a friend. We ate together. We also watched a movie. It was really fun.

homework: Write what you did yesterday (5 past tense sentences)
Conjugate: 마시다, 오다, 자다, 읽다 into past tense
Translate: 'I went to the cafe yesterday. I drank coffee and read a book.'

grammar.explanation: Verb stem + ㄹ/을 거예요 = will do / going to do. Use ㄹ after vowel, 을 after consonant.

reading.title: My Plans
reading.text: 이번 주말에 뭐 할 거예요? 친구랑 여행을 갈 거예요. 제주도에 갈 거예요. 정말 기대돼요!
reading.translation: What will you do this weekend? I will travel with my friend. We will go to Jeju Island. I'm really looking forward to it!

homework: Write your plans for next week using ㄹ/을 거예요 (6 sentences)
Use 고 싶어요 to express 3 things you want to do
Translate: 'Next week I will go to Seoul. I want to eat Korean food.'

grammar.pattern: Review: past tense, future tense, 아/어야 해요, 는 것을 좋아해요
grammar.explanation: Full review of Unit 4 grammar patterns with combined usage.

examples.meaning: I exercised yesterday. I will do it tomorrow too.
I have to exercise for my health.

reading.title: My Healthy Lifestyle
reading.text: 저는 건강을 위해 매일 운동해요. 어제는 수영을 했어요. 내일은 자전거를 탈 거예요. 건강한 음식도 먹어야 해요.
reading.translation: I exercise every day for my health. Yesterday I swam. Tomorrow I will ride a bicycle. I also need to eat healthy food.

homework: Write a paragraph about your health habits (past + present + future)
Review all vocabulary from Days 22–27
Mini quiz: conjugate 10 verbs into past and future tense

grammar.explanation: Expresses ability. Verb stem + -(으)ㄹ 수 있어요 = can do.

reading.title: Phone Call
reading.text: 여보세요? 네, 여보세요! 지금 통화할 수 있어요? 잠깐만요... 네, 괜찮아요. 내일 만날 수 있어요?
reading.translation: Hello? Yes, hello! Can you talk now? Just a moment... Yes, it's okay. Can we meet tomorrow?

homework: Write a phone call dialogue
Use -(으)ㄹ 수 있어요/없어요 in 5 sentences
Translate: 'I cannot come today. Can we meet tomorrow?'

vocabulary.meaning: How about? / What do you think?

grammar.explanation: Used to make suggestions or ask for opinions. Verb stem + -(으)ㄹ까요?

reading.title: Making Plans
reading.text: 이번 주말에 시간 있어요? 네, 있어요! 같이 영화 볼까요? 좋아요! 몇 시가 좋아요? 오후 두 시 어때요?
reading.translation: Are you free this weekend? Yes, I am! Shall we watch a movie together? Sounds good! What time is good? How about 2 PM?

homework: Write a dialogue making plans with a friend
Use -(으)ㄹ까요? for 3 suggestions
Translate: 'Shall we eat lunch together? Saturday at 12 PM, how about it?'

grammar.explanation: Used for polite instructions. Verb stem + -(으)세요.

reading.title: Where Is the Station?
reading.text: 지하철역이 어디예요? 직진하세요. 그 다음에 오른쪽으로 가세요. 큰 건물 옆에 있어요.
reading.translation: Where is the subway station? Go straight. Then turn right. It is next to the big building.

homework: Write directions from your home to the nearest store
Use -(으)세요 for each step
Translate: 'Turn left. Go straight. Cross the street. It is on the right.'

grammar.explanation: Connects two clauses or lists actions. Verb/adj stem + 고.

reading.title: My Best Friend
reading.text: 제 친구는 키가 크고 머리가 길어요. 안경을 써요. 성격이 좋고 재미있어요. 저는 그 친구를 정말 좋아해요.
reading.translation: My friend is tall and has long hair. She wears glasses. She has a good personality and is funny. I really like that friend.

homework: Describe a family member or friend in Korean (5–6 sentences)
Use 고 to connect at least 3 pairs of adjectives
Translate: 'My brother is short and has short hair. He is kind and smart.'

grammar.explanation: Conditional: verb stem + -(으)면 = if/when something happens.

examples.meaning: When you meet an elder, greet them.
If I have time, I study.
If it rains, I stay home.

reading.title: Korean Customs
reading.text: 한국에서는 어른에게 존댓말을 써야 해요. 밥을 먹을 때 어른이 먼저 드세요. 선물을 받으면 감사합니다 라고 해요.
reading.translation: In Korea, you must use polite speech with elders. When eating, elders eat first. When you receive a gift, you say 'Thank you.'

homework: Write 3 Korean cultural rules using -(으)면
Compare one Korean etiquette rule with your own culture
Translate: 'If you meet someone older, bow and greet them politely.'

grammar.explanation: Connects a reason to a result. Verb/adj stem + 아/어서.

examples.meaning: Because it's spring, there are many flowers.
Because it's hot, I drink water.
Because I'm tired, I sleep early.

reading.title: My Favorite Season
reading.text: 제가 제일 좋아하는 계절은 봄이에요. 봄에는 꽃이 많아서 예뻐요. 날씨도 따뜻해서 밖에 나가고 싶어요.
reading.translation: My favorite season is spring. In spring there are many flowers so it's beautiful. The weather is warm too so I want to go outside.

homework: Write about your favorite season and why
Use -(아/어)서 in 4 sentences
Translate: 'It's cold in winter so I stay home. It's fun in summer because I swim.'

grammar.explanation: Comprehensive review of all Unit 5 patterns with real-world application.

examples.meaning: If you have time, shall we eat together?
I can't go because I'm tired.

reading.title: Invitation
reading.text: 이번 토요일에 파티가 있어요. 초대하고 싶어요. 올 수 있어요? 시간이 있으면 꼭 와 주세요!
reading.translation: There is a party this Saturday. I want to invite you. Can you come? If you have time, please definitely come!

homework: Write an invitation message to a friend in Korean
Review all Unit 5 vocabulary (35 words)
Practice all grammar patterns from Days 29–34

grammar.pattern: Review: connecting sentences with 그리고, 하지만, 그래서, 그런데
grammar.explanation: Master discourse connectors for reading comprehension.

examples.meaning: I study Korean. So it's interesting.
I ate. But I'm still hungry.

reading.title: TOPIK Reading Strategy
reading.text: TOPIK 1 읽기에는 여러 종류의 문제가 있어요. 빈칸 채우기, 내용 이해, 주제 찾기가 있어요. 먼저 지문을 읽고 문제를 풀어요.
reading.translation: TOPIK 1 reading has various types of questions. There are fill-in-the-blank, content comprehension, and finding the main topic. First read the passage and then answer the questions.

homework: Practice identifying the main topic of 2 short Korean texts
Fill in blanks using appropriate connectors (그리고/하지만/그래서/그런데)
Translate a 5-sentence Korean paragraph to check comprehension

grammar.explanation: Practice answering 뭐, 어디, 언제, 왜, 어떻게 questions quickly and accurately.

examples.korean: Q: 어디에 가요? A: 학교에 가요.
examples.meaning: Q: Where are you going? A: To school.

examples.korean: Q: 왜 공부해요? A: 시험이 있어서요.
examples.meaning: Q: Why are you studying? A: Because I have an exam.

examples.korean: Q: 언제 자요? A: 밤 열 시에 자요.
examples.meaning: Q: When do you sleep? A: At 10 PM.

reading.title: TOPIK Listening Tip
reading.text: TOPIK 1 듣기는 천천히 들을 수 있어요. 키워드를 들어요. 그리고 상황을 생각해요. 답을 빨리 고르세요.
reading.translation: TOPIK 1 listening is manageable. Listen for keywords. And think about the situation. Choose your answer quickly.

homework: Write 5 Q&A pairs using different question words
Listen to a short Korean audio/video and write what you understood
Practice answering questions in under 10 seconds each

grammar.pattern: Negative forms review: 안 + verb, -지 않아요, 못 + verb
grammar.explanation: Review all negative forms. 안/지 않아요 = won't/don't. 못 = can't (ability).

examples.meaning: I can't go because I don't have time.

reading.title: Study Smart
reading.text: TOPIK을 준비할 때 어휘가 정말 중요해요. 매일 새로운 단어를 외워야 해요. 모르는 단어는 필기해요. 그리고 반복해서 사용해요.
reading.translation: When preparing for TOPIK, vocabulary is very important. You must memorize new words every day. Write down words you don't know. And use them repeatedly.

homework: Write the meaning of 10 TOPIK vocabulary words from memory
Write 5 sentences using negative forms
Translate: 'I can't choose. I don't understand. Please explain again.'

grammar.pattern: -(으)ㄴ/는 것 같아요 (it seems like / I think)
grammar.explanation: Expresses conjecture or softened opinion. Adjective/verb + -(으)ㄴ/는 것 같아요.

examples.meaning: It seems like it will rain.

reading.title: Sample TOPIK Passage
reading.text: 한국은 봄, 여름, 가을, 겨울 사계절이 있어요. 봄에는 꽃이 피고 따뜻해요. 여름에는 더워서 수영을 해요. 가을에는 단풍이 예뻐요. 겨울에는 눈이 와요.
reading.translation: Korea has four seasons: spring, summer, autumn, and winter. In spring flowers bloom and it's warm. In summer it's hot so people swim. In autumn the autumn leaves are beautiful. In winter it snows.

homework: Read the passage again and answer: (1) What season has flowers? (2) What do people do in summer? (3) What's special about autumn?
Write 3 sentences using -(으)ㄴ/는 것 같아요
Summarize the passage in 2 Korean sentences

grammar.pattern: Time adverbs: 아직, 벌써, 다시, 이미, 곧
grammar.explanation: Useful adverbs for TOPIK: 아직 = still/not yet, 벌써 = already, 곧 = soon, 이미 = already (done).

reading.title: A Letter from Korea
reading.text: 안녕하세요! 저는 지금 한국에 있어요. 한국에 온 지 벌써 2주가 됐어요. 아직 한국어가 어렵지만 매일 공부해요. 곧 잘할 수 있을 거예요!
reading.translation: Hello! I am in Korea right now. It's already been 2 weeks since I came to Korea. Korean is still difficult but I study every day. I will soon be able to do well!

homework: Identify all time adverbs in the reading and translate each sentence
Write 4 sentences using 아직, 벌써, 다시, 곧
Translate: 'I haven't finished yet. It's already 10 PM. I will sleep soon.'

grammar.pattern: Full grammar review — all patterns Days 1–40
grammar.explanation: Today is your mock grammar quiz day! Review all major patterns.

examples.korean: 내일 시험이 있어서 오늘 공부해야 해요.
examples.meaning: Because I have an exam tomorrow, I must study today.
If you study hard, you can pass.
When the exam is over, I will travel.

reading.title: Test Day Tips
reading.text: 시험 날은 일찍 일어나야 해요. 아침을 꼭 먹어요. 시험지를 받으면 먼저 문제를 전부 읽어요. 모르는 문제는 건너뛰고 나중에 다시 해요.
reading.translation: On test day you must wake up early. Be sure to eat breakfast. When you receive the test paper, first read all the questions. Skip questions you don't know and come back later.

homework: Complete this mini mock test: translate 10 sentences English→Korean
Write 5 sentences using patterns from multiple units
Study your 10 weakest vocabulary words from the course so far

grammar.pattern: Review of all grammar from Units 1–6
grammar.explanation: Today is a full review day. Write, read, and speak using all patterns you've learned.

examples.meaning: If you study consistently, your skills improve.
Don't give up. You can do it!
You've done well so far. Keep going!

reading.title: Halfway Reflection
reading.text: 벌써 42일이 됐어요! 많이 배웠어요. 처음에는 어려웠지만 지금은 조금 쉬워요. 꾸준히 공부하면 TOPIK 1에 합격할 수 있을 거예요. 파이팅!
reading.translation: It's already been 42 days! You've learned a lot. At first it was difficult but now it's a bit easier. If you study consistently, you will be able to pass TOPIK 1. Fighting!

homework: Write a self-reflection paragraph: what you've learned, what was hard, what improved
Full vocabulary review: quiz yourself on 50 random words from the course
Re-read Day 1 lesson and notice how much you've improved!

grammar.pattern: 에 vs 에서 (static location vs action location)
grammar.explanation: 에 = existence (있어요/없어요, destination). 에서 = where an action happens.

reading.title: At the Library
reading.text: 저는 오늘 도서관에서 공부했어요. 도서관은 학교 옆에 있어요. 아침 아홉 시부터 저녁 여섯 시까지 공부했어요.
reading.translation: Today I studied at the library. The library is next to the school. I studied from 9 AM until 6 PM.

homework: Write 5 sentences using 에 and 5 using 에서
Use 부터~까지 to describe your study schedule
Translate: 'I work at the cafe from 10 AM to 4 PM. The cafe is near my home.'

grammar.explanation: Use native Korean numbers (하나, 둘...) with counters. Numbers change slightly: 하나→한, 둘→두, 셋→세, 넷→네.

examples.meaning: Please give me one glass of water.

reading.title: At the Market
reading.text: 사과 다섯 개 주세요. 그리고 물 두 병도 주세요. 모두 얼마예요? 팔천 오백 원이에요.
reading.translation: Please give me 5 apples. And also 2 bottles of water. How much is everything? It's 8,500 won.

homework: Write what's in your bag/room using counters
Practice ordering items at a restaurant using the right counters
Translate: 'I have 3 books. I drank 2 cups of coffee today. There are 5 people.'

grammar.explanation: Used to express opinions. Noun + (이)라고 생각해요 or clause + 고 생각해요.

examples.meaning: I think it's a good idea.

reading.title: My Opinion on Korean Study
reading.text: 저는 한국어 공부가 중요하다고 생각해요. 매일 공부하는 것이 좋다고 생각해요. 처음에는 어렵지만 재미있어요.
reading.translation: I think studying Korean is important. I think studying every day is good. At first it's difficult but it's interesting.

homework: Write your opinion on 3 topics using -(이)라고 생각해요
Agree or disagree with these statements in Korean: '공부는 어렵다' / '한국 음식은 맛있다'
Translate: 'I think Korean culture is very interesting. I think I will pass TOPIK.'

grammar.pattern: A보다 B가 더 (A is more than B)
grammar.explanation: B + 보다 + A + 가/이 + 더 + adjective = A is more [adj] than B.

examples.meaning: The subway is faster than the bus.
I like spring more than summer.

reading.title: Which Is Better?
reading.text: 버스와 지하철 중에 뭐가 더 좋아요? 저는 지하철이 더 좋아요. 버스보다 빠르고 편해요. 하지만 버스가 더 싸요.
reading.translation: Between bus and subway, which is better? I prefer the subway. It's faster and more comfortable than the bus. But the bus is cheaper.

homework: Compare 3 pairs of things using A보다 B가 더
Use 제일/가장 to say what you like the most
Translate: 'Summer is hotter than spring. Winter is the coldest season. I like autumn the most.'

grammar.explanation: Expresses past experience. Verb stem + -(으)ㄴ 적이 있어요/없어요.

examples.meaning: I have never tried eating kimchi.

reading.title: My Korean Experiences
reading.text: 저는 한국 음식을 먹어본 적이 있어요. 비빔밥이 제일 맛있었어요. 하지만 한국에 간 적은 없어요. 언젠가 꼭 가고 싶어요!
reading.translation: I have tried eating Korean food before. Bibimbap was the most delicious. But I have never been to Korea. Someday I definitely want to go!

homework: Write 5 experiences you have had and 3 you haven't
Use -(으)ㄴ 적이 있어요/없어요 for each
Translate: 'Have you been to Seoul? I have been there once. Have you tried tteokbokki? I haven't tried it yet.'

grammar.explanation: Expresses intention. Verb stem + -(으)려고 해요 = I intend to / I'm planning to.

examples.meaning: I'm planning to go to Korea next year.
I'm studying hard to pass TOPIK.
I intend to wake up early tomorrow.

reading.title: My TOPIK Goals
reading.text: 저는 올해 TOPIK 1에 합격하려고 해요. 그래서 매일 한국어를 공부해요. 목표를 이루기 위해 열심히 노력할 거예요!
reading.translation: I intend to pass TOPIK 1 this year. So I study Korean every day. I will work hard to achieve my goal!

homework: Write 5 personal intentions/plans using -(으)려고 해요
Write your Korean study goal in Korean
Translate: 'I'm planning to take TOPIK next month. I intend to study vocabulary every day.'

grammar.pattern: Review: 에 vs 에서, counters, -(으)ㄴ 적, -(으)려고 해요, 보다 더
grammar.explanation: Full review of Unit 7 advanced patterns.

examples.meaning: Have you ever studied at the library?
I'm planning to take the subway rather than the bus.

examples.korean: 사과 세 개하고 물 두 병 주세요.
examples.meaning: Please give me 3 apples and 2 bottles of water.

reading.title: You're Almost There!
reading.text: 49일 동안 정말 열심히 했어요! 문법도 배웠고 어휘도 배웠어요. 이제 TOPIK 1 시험을 볼 준비가 거의 됐어요. 계속 노력하세요!
reading.translation: You've worked really hard for 49 days! You've learned grammar and vocabulary. Now you are almost ready to take the TOPIK 1 exam. Keep working hard!

homework: Full grammar quiz: use 10 different patterns in 10 sentences
Vocabulary review: 50 random words from all units
Write a paragraph about your Korean learning journey

grammar.explanation: 은/는, 이/가, 을/를, 에, 에서, 로/으로, 도, 부터, 까지, 한테, 처럼 — practice using each one correctly.

examples.korean: 저는 학교에서 친구한테 한국어를 가르쳐 줘요.
examples.meaning: I teach Korean to my friend at school.
I go from home to school by bus.
I work from 9 AM to 6 PM.

reading.title: Review Passage 1
reading.text: 저는 매일 아침 여섯 시에 일어나요. 버스로 학교에 가요. 학교에서 한국어를 공부해요. 저녁에는 가족과 밥을 먹어요. 건강을 위해 운동도 해요.
reading.translation: I wake up every morning at 6 AM. I go to school by bus. I study Korean at school. In the evening I eat with my family. I also exercise for my health.

homework: Translate the reading passage into your native language without looking
Write a similar paragraph about yourself
Review and test yourself on 60 vocabulary words from Units 1–4

grammar.explanation: 아요/어요, 았/었어요, -(으)ㄹ 거예요, 아/어야 해요, -(으)ㄹ 수 있어요, -(으)려고 해요, -(으)ㄴ 적이 있어요

examples.korean: 어제 공부했어요. 오늘도 공부해요. 내일도 공부할 거예요.
examples.meaning: I studied yesterday. I study today. I will study tomorrow too.

examples.korean: TOPIK에 합격한 적이 있어요? 아직 없어요. 이번에 합격하려고 해요.
examples.meaning: Have you passed TOPIK? Not yet. I'm planning to pass this time.

examples.korean: 열심히 공부해야 해요. 할 수 있어요!
examples.meaning: I have to study hard. I can do it!

reading.title: Review Passage 2
reading.text: 저는 한국어를 6개월 공부했어요. 처음에는 어려웠어요. 하지만 꾸준히 공부했어요. 이제 한국 드라마를 조금 알아들을 수 있어요. TOPIK 1에 합격하려고 해요!
reading.translation: I have studied Korean for 6 months. At first it was difficult. But I studied consistently. Now I can understand Korean dramas a little. I'm planning to pass TOPIK 1!

homework: Review and test yourself on 60 vocabulary words from Units 5–8
Write 5 sentences using different tenses and modalities
Translate the reading passage without looking at the translation

grammar.explanation: Focus on catching: WHO (누가), WHAT (무엇을), WHERE (어디서), WHEN (언제), WHY (왜), HOW (어떻게).

examples.meaning: Where do we meet? → We meet at the cafe.

examples.korean: 언제 출발해요? → 오후 두 시에 출발해요.
examples.meaning: When do we leave? → We leave at 2 PM.

reading.title: Listening Test Practice Script
reading.text: 남자: 내일 시간 있어요? 여자: 네, 있어요. 왜요? 남자: 같이 영화 볼까요? 여자: 좋아요! 몇 시에 만날까요? 남자: 오후 세 시 어때요?
reading.translation: Man: Are you free tomorrow? Woman: Yes, I am. Why? Man: Shall we watch a movie together? Woman: Sounds good! What time shall we meet? Man: How about 3 PM?

homework: Read the dialogue aloud 3 times and memorize it
Answer: (1) What are they planning? (2) When will they meet? (3) Who suggested the activity?
Find a TOPIK 1 sample listening audio online and practice

grammar.explanation: For fill-in-blank: look at the surrounding context. For 'not matching': eliminate clearly wrong options. For main idea: look at the first and last sentences.

examples.meaning: I exercise every day. _____ I am healthy. (Answer: 그래서/So)
It rained. _____ I brought an umbrella. (Answer: 그래서/So)
The food is delicious. _____ it's too expensive. (Answer: 하지만/But)

reading.title: TOPIK Practice Passage
reading.text: 민지는 대학생이에요. 매일 아침 일찍 학교에 가요. 학교에서 열심히 공부해요. 저녁에는 아르바이트를 해요. 그래서 항상 바빠요. 하지만 행복해요.
reading.translation: Minji is a university student. She goes to school early every morning. She studies hard at school. In the evening she works a part-time job. So she is always busy. But she is happy.

homework: Answer: (1) What does Minji do in the evening? (2) Why is she always busy? (3) Is she happy or sad?
Find the connector words in the passage and explain why each is used
Write a similar passage about your own life

grammar.explanation: Review the most commonly confused patterns: 에 vs 에서, 이에요 vs 있어요, 안 vs 못, 은/는 vs 이/가.

examples.korean: 집에 있어요 (home location) vs 집에서 쉬어요 (action at home)
examples.meaning: 에 = where it IS, 에서 = where action HAPPENS

examples.korean: 안 가요 (choice not to go) vs 못 가요 (unable to go)
저는 학생이에요 (I AM a student) vs 학교에 있어요 (I am AT school)

reading.title: Grammar Pitfall Review
reading.text: 저는 지금 카페에 있어요. 카페에서 공부해요. 오늘 피곤해서 집에 가고 싶어요. 하지만 시험이 있어서 공부해야 해요. 안 피곤한 척해요!
reading.translation: I am at the cafe right now. I am studying at the cafe. I'm tired today so I want to go home. But I have an exam so I have to study. I'm pretending not to be tired!

homework: Write 3 example sentences for each confusing grammar pair above
Identify and correct 5 intentional errors: '학교에서 있어요', '못 안 가요', '저는 학생이 있어요'
Review your top 5 personal weak spots from this course

grammar.explanation: Today simulate a real TOPIK exam. Do your best within time limits.

examples.meaning: Don't be nervous. You can do it.

reading.title: Full Mock Reading Passage
reading.text: 제 친구 민수는 요리사예요. 매일 아침 일찍 일어나요. 시장에서 신선한 재료를 사요. 그리고 레스토랑에서 요리해요. 음식이 정말 맛있어요. 손님들이 아주 좋아해요.
reading.translation: My friend Minsu is a chef. He wakes up early every morning. He buys fresh ingredients at the market. And he cooks at a restaurant. The food is really delicious. The customers like it very much.

homework: Full 30-question mock test: 15 vocabulary/grammar, 15 reading comprehension (write your own questions)
Time yourself: aim to complete in 40 minutes
Score yourself and note areas that need more review

grammar.explanation: Review each wrong answer from the mock test. Understand WHY it was wrong and write the correct version.

examples.korean: Wrong: 학교에서 있어요. Right: 학교에 있어요.
examples.meaning: Use 에 for location/existence, 에서 for actions.

examples.korean: Wrong: 내일 먹었어요. Right: 내일 먹을 거예요.
Wrong: 음악이 좋아해요. Right: 음악을 좋아해요.

reading.title: Learning From Mistakes
reading.text: 시험에서 틀린 문제를 복습해요. 왜 틀렸는지 분석해요. 그리고 다시 공부해요. 실수에서 배우는 것이 중요해요. 다음에는 더 잘할 수 있어요!
reading.translation: I review the questions I got wrong on the exam. I analyze why I got them wrong. Then I study again. It's important to learn from mistakes. Next time I can do even better!

homework: Analyze every wrong answer from Mock Test (Day 55)
Rewrite each wrong answer correctly with explanation
Study the top 3 grammar points you struggled with most

grammar.explanation: 항상 (always), 보통 (usually), 가끔 (sometimes), 자주 (often), 절대 (never) — master these for TOPIK.

reading.title: Daily Habits
reading.text: 저는 보통 일찍 일어나요. 항상 아침을 먹어요. 자주 한국어를 공부해요. 가끔 친구를 만나요. 절대 게임을 안 해요. (그건 거짓말이에요!)
reading.translation: I usually wake up early. I always eat breakfast. I often study Korean. I sometimes meet friends. I never play games. (That's a lie!)

homework: Write a paragraph about your habits using all 5 frequency adverbs
Memorize the full list of 50 key TOPIK vocabulary words
Test yourself: 50 flashcards, aim for 90% accuracy

grammar.pattern: Top 20 TOPIK 1 grammar patterns — speed review
grammar.explanation: 1.이에요/예요 2.은/는 3.이/가 4.을/를 5.에 6.에서 7.아/어요 8.았/었어요 9.ㄹ/을 거예요 10.아/어야 해요 11.ㄹ/을 수 있어요 12.고 13.아/어서 14.(으)면 15.아/어 주세요 16.ㄹ/을까요 17.ㄴ 적이 있어요 18.(으)려고 해요 19.보다 더 20.ㄴ/는 것 같아요

examples.meaning: Because it rained, I brought an umbrella.

examples.korean: 한국에 간 적이 있으면 이야기해 주세요.
examples.meaning: If you have been to Korea, please tell me.

examples.korean: 열심히 하면 합격할 수 있을 것 같아요.
examples.meaning: If you work hard, I think you will be able to pass.

reading.title: Final Grammar Check
reading.text: 오늘 모든 문법을 복습했어요. 어려운 것도 있었지만 이해할 수 있었어요. 내일부터 시험 준비를 완벽하게 할 거예요. 최선을 다할 것 같아요!
reading.translation: Today I reviewed all the grammar. There were difficult things but I could understand them. From tomorrow I will prepare perfectly for the exam. I think I will do my best!

homework: Write one sentence for each of the 20 grammar patterns above
Time yourself: complete in under 30 minutes
Highlight any patterns that felt uncertain and review them

grammar.explanation: TOPIK 1 has 2 sections: Listening (30 questions, 40 min) and Reading (40 questions, 60 min). Score 80+ to pass Level 1, 140+ for Level 2.

reading.title: Exam Day Advice
reading.text: 내일 시험이에요! 오늘 밤 충분히 자세요. 아침을 꼭 드세요. 시험장에 일찍 도착하세요. 침착하게 문제를 푸세요. 수고했어요. 합격할 수 있어요!
reading.translation: The exam is tomorrow! Sleep enough tonight. Be sure to eat breakfast. Arrive at the exam hall early. Solve questions calmly. Well done. You can pass!

homework: Complete a full simulated TOPIK 1 exam (use official past papers if available)
Sleep early tonight!
Review test-taking strategies one more time

grammar.explanation: Today celebrate your achievement! You've covered all TOPIK 1 content. Review whatever feels weakest.

examples.meaning: You worked really hard for 60 days!
Your Korean skills must have improved a lot.
Keep studying from now on. Fighting!

reading.title: 🎉 Congratulations! 🎉
reading.text: 축하합니다! 60일 동안 정말 열심히 공부했어요. 처음에는 어려웠지만 끝까지 했어요. 정말 대단해요! 이제 TOPIK 1 시험을 볼 준비가 됐어요. 자신감을 가지세요. 할 수 있어요! 앞으로도 한국어 공부를 계속하세요. 화이팅! 🇰🇷
reading.translation: Congratulations! You studied really hard for 60 days. At first it was difficult but you made it to the end. That's truly amazing! Now you are ready to take the TOPIK 1 exam. Have confidence. You can do it! Keep studying Korean going forward. Fighting! 🇰🇷

homework: Write a letter to yourself about your Korean learning journey
List 10 things you can now say in Korean that you couldn't on Day 1
Plan your next Korean learning goal: TOPIK 2? Korean drama without subtitles? A trip to Korea?
